title: rosa gallica f. ruralis
intro: Rosa gallica f. ruralis, also known as the French rose or rural gallica rose, is a species of rose plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It is a fragrant, single-petaled rose with a rich history in traditional medicine, valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and digestive problems, due to its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. Rosa gallica f. ruralis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to promote relaxation, ease respiratory issues, and support overall well-being.
